title: Ensure that the kube-proxy metrics service is bound to localhost

input_row.title: Ensure that the kube-proxy metrics service is bound to localhost
input_row.description: Do not bind the kube-proxy metrics port to non-loopback addresses.
input_row.rationale: kube-proxy has two APIs which provided access to information about the service and can be bound to network ports. The metrics API service includes endpoints ( /metrics and /configz ) which disclose information about the configuration and operation of kube-proxy. These endpoints should not be exposed to untrusted networks as they do not support encryption or authentication to restrict access to the data they provide.
input_row.audit: review the start-up flags provided to kube proxy Run the following command on each node: ps -ef | grep -i kube-proxy Ensure that the --metrics-bind-address parameter is not set to a value other than 127.0.0.1. From the output of this command gather the location specified in the -- config parameter. Review any file stored at that location and ensure that it does not specify a value other than 127.0.0.1 for metricsBindAddress .
input_row.remediation: Modify or remove any values which bind the metrics service to a non-localhost address
input_row.impact: 3rd party services which try to access metrics or configuration information related to kube-proxy will require access to the localhost interface of the node.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit mentions reviewing the start-up flags for kube-proxy, which can be done using Kubernetes API or kubectl commands.
- The specific parameter `--metrics-bind-address` can be checked through the kube-proxy configuration.
- The control is related to a standard Kubernetes service property, specifically the kube-proxy metrics service binding.
- The audit does not require SSH/RDP access to instances, nor does it require subjective visual review.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_kube_proxy_metrics_bind_address_localhost

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the command `kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l k8s-app=kube-proxy -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].args}'` to retrieve the start-up flags for kube-proxy.
- Check for the presence of the `--metrics-bind-address` flag and ensure it is set to `127.0.0.1`.
- If the `--config` flag is present, retrieve the configuration file specified and check the `metricsBindAddress` setting to ensure it is also set to `127.0.0.1`.
- Pass criteria: The `--metrics-bind-address` must be `127.0.0.1` and the `metricsBindAddress` in the configuration file must also be `127.0.0.1`. If either is set to a non-localhost address, it fails compliance.
